steps.title: Identify the key signal challenging the conventional view
steps.content: The key finding is that participants from ten vastly different cultures ranked odors similarly “regardless of where they lived.” The word “regardless” signals that culture and location did not determine preferences, which directly contradicts the conventional wisdom that culture mediates—or determines—olfactory valence.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: Because participants ranked odors similarly “regardless of where they lived,” the conventional view that culture mediates valence is undermined. Choice D directly expresses this—the standard view “may be unsound.” The other choices reverse the relationship (A), claim unsupported specifics about agreement patterns (B), or misidentify the issue as objective measurement rather than culture's role (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
